title: Gap Map Matrix
description: Each field placed by how much technical activity it draws against how much policy attention it holds. Distance from the diagonal is the gap.
note: Circle size = frontier score. Illustrative, drawn from the cited sources.

topics.label: AI Governance
topics.insight: Rules, standards, and institutions are forming quickly, and policy attention roughly tracks technical progress.

topics.label: Compute Governance
topics.insight: Concentrated computing capacity is advancing faster than the governance frameworks built around it.

topics.label: Military AI
topics.insight: Rapid technical advancement in military AI is outpacing policy development and public institutional focus.

topics.label: Cybersecurity
topics.insight: A mature field where technical practice and policy attention are relatively well aligned.

topics.label: Semiconductor Policy
topics.insight: Industrial strategy has begun to catch up with a concentrated, strategically vital technical frontier.

topics.label: Defence Innovation
topics.insight: Institutions increasingly exist to connect emerging technology with collective security needs.

topics.label: Arctic Security
topics.insight: A widening field where security, climate, and infrastructure meet, still read by many as regional.

topics.label: Undersea Infrastructure
topics.insight: Strategic importance is easy to miss until a disruption makes the dependency visible.

topics.label: Export Controls
topics.insight: A policy lever moving ahead of shared technical understanding of its second-order effects.

topics.label: Biosecurity
topics.insight: Convergent life-science and security concerns are drawing attention across separate communities.

topics.label: Critical Minerals
topics.insight: Supply concentration ties resource policy to security and industrial strategy at once.

topics.label: Space Security
topics.insight: Capabilities are expanding faster than the norms and institutions meant to govern them.
